rationale: The cited paper appears highly relevant to the citing paper’s core topic: both study assortment/display arrangement effects on processing fluency and downstream choice outcomes (citing note: arrangement-driven disfluency and search; cited note: horizontal/vertical orientation, fluency, perceived variety, and choice). The citing paper also reports robustness across vertical/horizontal display formats, making the 2016 orientation paper a plausible direct theoretical/comparison foundation rather than an incidental mention; the DOI match is exact.
